Okay, hereŌĆÖs my summary:

Delirium tremens is caused by abrupt cessation of chronic heavy alcohol use, triggering neurological dysfunction due to disrupted neurotransmitter balance (GABA inhibition reduced, glutamate excitation enhanced). This leads to hallmark signs like agitation, disorientation, visual/hallucinatory disturbances, and life-threatening autonomic fluctuations upon stopping alcohol abruptly.
